description: Improve OpenTabs CLI user-friendliness and ergonomics — dynamic audit table columns, doctor OpenCode config check, bare command default to status, audit header fix, plugin resolve parallelization, indent function cleanup

userStories.title: Make bare `opentabs` (no args) run `opentabs status` instead of showing help
userStories.description: As a user, I want running `opentabs` with no arguments to show server status so that I get immediately useful information without having to remember the `status` subcommand
acceptanceCriteria: Running the CLI with no arguments delegates to the status command handler
If the server is not running, the status command's 'Server not running' error is shown (not the help text)
Running `opentabs --help` still shows the full help text with all commands
userStories.notes: Edit `platform/cli/src/cli.ts`. Commander does not have a built-in 'default command' feature, but there are two approaches: (1) Add a `.action()` on the root program that delegates to `handleStatus` when no subcommand is provided, or (2) check `process.argv.length` before parsing and inject 'status' if no subcommand is present. Approach (1) is cleaner — register an action on the root `program` that calls the same `handleStatus` function used by the status command. The action receives options and the command object. Import `handleStatus` (it may need to be exported from `platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts`). The root action is only called when no subcommand matches. Note: Commander calls the root action when no subcommand is provided, so `opentabs --port 3000` with no subcommand would also trigger status on that port — which is desirable behavior.

userStories.title: Make audit table tool column width dynamic
userStories.description: As a user, I want the audit table to handle long tool names without breaking alignment so that all columns remain readable regardless of tool name length
acceptanceCriteria: Tool column width is computed as max(header length, longest tool name in the current result set) + 2 padding
Tool names longer than the previous fixed 30-char limit display correctly without column overlap
Short tool names still align properly with appropriate padding
The separator line adjusts to match the total computed row width
userStories.notes: Edit `platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts`. Currently `COL_TOOL = 30` is hardcoded at line 91. Change `printAuditTable` to compute the tool column width dynamically: `const colTool = Math.max(COL_TOOL_MIN, ...entries.map(e => e.tool.length)) + 2` where `COL_TOOL_MIN` could be the header text length ('Tool'.length). Then use `colTool` instead of `COL_TOOL` in the header, separator, and row formatting. Keep COL_TIME, COL_STATUS, COL_DURATION as fixed since those values are predictable.

userStories.title: Fix audit table 'OK' header to say 'Status'
userStories.description: As a user, I want the audit table status column header to clearly indicate what the column represents so that I can immediately understand the checkmark/cross icons
acceptanceCriteria: The audit table header shows a clearer column name instead of 'OK' (either blank, or a short word that fits the column width)
The column width COL_STATUS is adjusted if needed to fit the new header
Checkmark and cross icons still display correctly with proper color coding
userStories.notes: Edit `platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts`. At line 92, `COL_STATUS = 4` and the header at line 98 uses `'OK'.padEnd(COL_STATUS)`. The column shows checkmark/cross icons, so the header 'OK' is misleading — it implies a boolean yes/no answer but the checkmark represents 'success'. Options: (1) Keep 'OK' but it's standard in some CLIs, (2) Use empty string '' as header since the icons are self-explanatory and users learn the pattern quickly, (3) Use a single unicode icon in the header as a legend. Option (1) is actually reasonable — 'OK' is a well-understood convention. But the actual issue is that COL_STATUS=4 is barely enough for the icon + padding. Consider widening to accommodate a clearer header. The simplest fix: keep it minimal — 'OK' is fine for this column. Alternatively, widen COL_STATUS to 6 and use header '' (empty) since the green checkmark / red cross are universally understood. The real value of this story is ensuring the table is clean.

userStories.title: Add OpenCode to doctor MCP client config check
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs doctor` to check my OpenCode MCP configuration so that it catches missing OpenCode setup alongside Claude Code and Cursor
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor checks for `opencode.json` in the current working directory
The check looks for an 'opentabs' entry inside the 'mcp' field (matching OpenCode's config format)
If OpenCode config is found with opentabs configured, doctor reports it as passing
If no MCP client has opentabs configured (including OpenCode), the existing warning is shown
Doctor tests in platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.test.ts cover the new OpenCode check
userStories.notes: Edit `platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts`. The `defaultMcpClientLocations()` function at line 235-238 returns an array of client locations. Add OpenCode: `{ name: 'OpenCode', path: join(process.cwd(), 'opencode.json') }`. The `checkMcpClientConfig` function at line 240-268 iterates these locations and checks for an `mcpServers.opentabs` entry. OpenCode's config format is different — it uses `mcp.opentabs` (not `mcpServers.opentabs`). The check at line 249-255 currently only looks for `mcpServers.opentabs`. For OpenCode, add an additional check: if `parsed` has an `mcp` field that is an object containing `opentabs`, that also counts as configured. Update the doctor.test.ts to add test cases for the OpenCode config path and format.

userStories.title: Parallelize plugin name resolution for shorthand installs
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs plugin install slack` to resolve the package name faster by checking npm candidates in parallel instead of sequentially
acceptanceCriteria: For shorthand names, both official and community npm candidates are checked concurrently
The official scope candidate is still preferred over the community one when both exist
If only one candidate exists, it is used regardless of scope
If neither candidate exists, the same 'not found' error with tried candidates is shown
Plugin install for already-qualified names (scoped or prefixed) still works without extra lookups
userStories.notes: Edit `platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts`. Currently `resolvePackageName` at line 102-110 calls `packageExistsOnNpm` sequentially for each candidate. `packageExistsOnNpm` at line 90-93 uses `spawnSync` — this blocks the event loop. To parallelize: (1) Create an async `packageExistsOnNpmAsync` using the existing `spawnProcessAsync` helper (line 51-66), (2) Change `resolvePackageName` to async, (3) Use `Promise.all` to check all candidates concurrently, (4) Return the first candidate by priority that exists. The callers `handlePluginInstall` and `handlePluginRemove` are already async so this is straightforward. Also update `handlePluginSearch`'s direct lookup to use async. Note: `resolvePackageName` is also called from `handlePluginRemove` — update that call site too.

userStories.title: Clean up the no-op indent function in start.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the indent function to be simplified since both branches of the ternary produce the same result, making the code clearer
acceptanceCriteria: The indent function at line 149-153 of start.ts is simplified to remove the redundant ternary
The MCP client config JSON output is unchanged (identical visual output)
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts` line 149-153. The current indent function is:
```
const indent = (json: string, prefix: string): string =>
  json
    .split('\n')
    .map((line, i) => (i === 0 ? `${prefix}${line}` : `${prefix}${line}`))
    .join('\n');
```
Both branches of the ternary `(i === 0 ? ... : ...)` produce identical output `${prefix}${line}`. Simplify to:
```
const indent = (json: string, prefix: string): string =>
  json
    .split('\n')
    .map(line => `${prefix}${line}`)
    .join('\n');
```
This is dead logic cleanup — the visual output is unchanged.

userStories.title: Add `--verbose` flag to `opentabs plugin list` to show tool names
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which tools each plugin provides when listing plugins so that I can quickly find the right tool name without having to check documentation
acceptanceCriteria: A new `--verbose` (or `-v`) flag is added to `opentabs plugin list`
When --verbose is used and the server is running, each plugin entry shows its tool names indented below the plugin line
Without --verbose, the output is unchanged (just tool count)
The verbose output works in offline mode too (reading from dist/tools.json for local plugins)
Help text for `opentabs plugin list` includes the --verbose flag and an example
userStories.notes: Edit `platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts`. For the online path (server running), the health endpoint already returns `pluginDetails` with a `tools` field (array of tool name strings) — see `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts` line 139-143 for the HealthPluginDetail type that includes `tools: string[]`. Use this data to print tool names when --verbose is set. For the offline path, `readLocalPluginInfo` at line 438-467 already reads `dist/tools.json` — extend it to also return tool names (from the `tools` array in tools.json, each entry has a `name` field). For npm plugins in offline mode, tool names are unavailable — just show the count. Format: indent tool names with 4 extra spaces under each plugin entry, e.g. `      slack_send_message, slack_list_channels`. Add the flag: `.option('-v, --verbose', 'Show tool names for each plugin')`.
